title: Columbaria atropurpurea
intro: Columbaria atropurpurea, also known as Purple Columba, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its purple flowers and edible berries. The health benefits of Columbaria atropurpurea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional herbal remedies. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Columbaria atropurpurea include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, often used to support immune function and alleviate digestive issues.

It is a shrub native to the eastern part of Australia, specifically in the states of Queensland and New South Wales.
